mechanism_narrative: A sustained low unemployment rate below 4% indicates tight labor markets that boost consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: When UNRATE rises above 4% and the 20-day forward return becomes negative in at least 60% of subsequent instances, or Rank IC between UNRATE level and forward returns turns negative over a 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2% and is rising over a 3-month period, it signals monetary tightening that increases discount rates and depresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If triggered instances yield positive 20-day forward returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the average return difference between triggered and non-triggered periods is not statistically significant (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation rises above the federal funds rate, real interest rates become negative, eroding real returns on savings and signaling excessive accommodation that will require future tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the joint condition (CPI > Fed Funds) produces positive 60-day forward returns in more than 40% of instances, or if the spread between CPI and Fed Funds has no predictive rank correlation with returns over a 3-year rolling window (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: In a high-growth regime where GDP growth exceeds 3% year-over-year, further acceleration signals overheating and future contraction, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If GDPC1 YoY > 3% is followed by positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of quarters, or if the sign of the signal flips to positive in a low-growth regime (GDPC1 YoY < 2%) and the mechanism does not produce negative returns.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production signals increasing economic output and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon as markets react to production data releases.
failure_condition: If the 5-day forward return following positive INDPRO changes is not significantly positive (average return < 0% with p < 0.10), or if the signal fails to produce a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 over a 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts fall below 1.2 million annualized units, it signals weakening demand and impending recession, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If the signal (HOUST < 1200) produces positive 60-day returns in more than 30% of instances, or if the hit rate of negative returns is less than 60%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment growth (above 200k/month) combined with an already tight labor market (unemployment below 4%) signals overheating, wage inflation, and impending Fed tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If joint condition produces positive 20-day returns in more than 40% of cases, or if the interaction term (PAYEMS growth * low UNRATE dummy) has no significant predictive power in a regression (t-stat < 1.96) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a low inflation regime (CPI year-over-year below 2%), a gradual rise in inflation signals strengthening demand without immediate monetary tightening, which is positive for equity returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If triggered instances yield negative 20-day returns in more than 50% of cases, or if the regime condition (CPI < 2%) fails to produce a positive average return differential versus the high-inflation regime over a 3-year period.
